hero.heading: ☁️ AWS - Cloud Building Blocks
hero.subtitle: Overview of Amazon Web Services: compute, storage, networking, and managed services to build modern applications.
hero.whyLearn: AWS is the largest cloud provider with a broad set of services; understanding core services is essential for cloud-native development.

storytelling.problem: On-prem infra takes weeks to provision and is hard to scale. Developers wait days for dev environments.
storytelling.solution: AWS provides on-demand virtual infrastructure and managed services so teams can provision resources in minutes and scale automatically.
storytelling.analogy: Think of AWS as renting specialized factories on demand instead of building your own; spin them up when you need them and tear them down when done.
storytelling.realWorldExample: Startups launch new features by provisioning managed databases, object storage, and serverless functions without managing hardware.
storytelling.deepDive: Key areas: Compute (EC2, Lambda), Storage (S3, EBS), Databases (RDS, DynamoDB), Networking (VPC), and Observability (CloudWatch).

architectureFlow.title: Design Services
architectureFlow.details: Pick managed services (Lambda, RDS) to reduce operational load.

architectureFlow.title: Secure & IAM
architectureFlow.description: Use IAM roles and policies to grant least privilege
architectureFlow.details: Design role-based access for services and users, enable MFA for administrative accounts.

architectureFlow.title: Deploy & Scale
architectureFlow.description: Use AutoScaling, Load Balancers, and managed services
architectureFlow.details: Combine ASG, ALB/NLB, and RDS read replicas for high availability.

architectureFlow.title: Monitor & Operate
architectureFlow.details: Set up dashboards, alarms, and automated remediation for common failures.

beginner.description: Object storage for static assets and backups.
Basics of subnets, security groups, and route tables.
Users, roles, policies, and best practices.

intermediate.description: Managed relational vs NoSQL choices and scaling patterns.
Use CodePipeline, CodeBuild, or integrate GitHub Actions with AWS.

advanced.description: Right-size resources and use reserved/spot instances.

intro.what: Amazon Web Services (AWS) is a comprehensive cloud platform offering over 200 fully featured services from data centers globally.
intro.why: AWS provides elasticity, managed services, and global infrastructure which help teams move faster and operate at scale.
intro.industry_importance: AWS is the market leader in cloud computing and a core skill for cloud and DevOps engineers.

beginnerMistakes.title: Overprovisioning Resources
beginnerMistakes.description: Launching large instances by default increases cost.
beginnerMistakes.howToFix: Start small, use autoscaling, and monitor usage.

productionSection.cicd: Integrate Terraform or CloudFormation with CI to manage infrastructure changes safely.
productionSection.monitoring: Use CloudWatch metrics and alarms; centralize logs in CloudWatch Logs or external services.

labs.title: Deploy a Static Site to S3
labs.description: Host a static website on S3 with CloudFront CDN

steps.title: Create S3 bucket
steps.instruction: Enable static website hosting and upload files

labs.expectedOutput: Website served from CloudFront with HTTPS

interviewQuestions.question: When would you use Lambda over EC2?
interviewQuestions.answer: Use Lambda for short-lived, event-driven workloads where you don't want to manage servers; choose EC2 for long-running or highly specialized workloads.

core_aws_commands.description: Set up AWS CLI credentials and default region
List S3 buckets you can access

deployment_tips: Use IAM roles instead of long-lived access keys whenever possible
Prefer managed services like S3, RDS, and Lambda to reduce ops overhead
Tag resources consistently for cost tracking and automation
